news_summary: Advancements in genetic technology, neuroscience, virology, and AI are driving breakthroughs in nutrition, brain function, infectious disease understanding, and antibiotic discovery.
news_summary_fr: Les progrès réalisés dans les domaines de la génétique, des neurosciences, de la virologie et de l'intelligence artificielle sont à l'origine de percées majeures en matière de nutrition, de fonctionnement du cerveau, de compréhension des maladies infectieuses et de découverte d'antibiotiques.
news_summary_es: Los avances en tecnología genética, neurociencia, virología e inteligencia artificial están impulsando avances revolucionarios en nutrición, función cerebral, comprensión de las enfermedades infecciosas y descubrimiento de antibióticos.

articles.title: Genetic technologies to enhance crop nutritional value under climate change
articles.summary: Nature, Published online: 24 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10593-6This Review reflects on the joint power of genetic technologies, including untapped CRISPR–Cas techniques to combat hidden hunger and improve crop resilience, and argues in favour of their combined use to overcome these societal challenges.
articles.content:
Passarelli, S. et al. Global estimation of dietary micronutrient inadequacies: a modelling analysis. Lancet Glob. Health 12, e1590–e1599 (2024). This modelling analysis combines micronutrient intake data from 31 countries with publicly available data from the Global Dietary Database, to estimate the prevalence of inadequate nutrient intakes for over 99% of the global population.

Stevens, G. A. et al. Micronutrient deficiencies among preschool-aged children and women of reproductive age worldwide: a pooled analysis of individual-level data from population-representative surveys. Lancet Glob. Health 10, e1590–e1599 (2022).

Bouis, H. et al. Biofortification: future challenges for a newly emerging technology to improve nutrition security sustainably. Curr. Dev. Nutr. 8, 104478 (2024).

FAO, IFAD, UNICEF, WFP & WHO. The State of Food Security and Nutrition in the World 2025: Addressing High Food Price Inflation for Food Security and Nutrition (FAO, 2025).

Headey, D. et al. Impacts of COVID-19 on childhood malnutrition and nutrition-related mortality. Lancet 396, 519–521 (2020).

This paper presents universal scientific targets to reach a global food systems transformation aimed at sustainable health of people and planet; the reference below is an update that adds a global overview on equity in food systems.

Carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) levels this century will alter the protein, micronutrients, and vitamin content of rice grains with potential health consequences for the poorest rice-dependent countries. Sci. Adv. 4, eaaq1012 (2018). This study covers multi-year, multi-location free-air CO 2 enrichment experiments for 18 genetically diverse rice lines, providing a first example of a marked reduction of multiple micronutrients in a staple crop subjected to higher concentrations of CO 2 .

Li, G. et al. Integrated biotechnological and AI innovations for crop improvement. Nature 643, 925–937 (2025). This paper proposes the integration of omics technologies, genome editing, protein design and high-throughput phenotyping, supported by artificial-intelligence-enabled tools, to face challenges imposed by global climate change and contribute to food security.

articles.reasoning: The article discusses the use of advanced genetic technologies, including CRISPR-Cas, to biofortify crops and enhance their nutritional value, addressing global micronutrient deficiencies and hidden hunger exacerbated by climate change. This represents a significant, tangible advancement with broad societal benefits for global nutrition security and health.
articles.personality_title: New genetic tools improve crop nutrition to fight hidden hunger and climate challenges
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Many people around the world do not get enough vitamins and minerals in their diets, a problem called hidden hunger. Climate change makes this worse by lowering the nutrition in staple crops like rice and wheat.

**What happened** – Scientists are using advanced genetic technologies, especially CRISPR-Cas gene editing, to make crops healthier. These techniques help add important nutrients like vitamins B, C, and A directly into plants such as rice, wheat, bananas, and tomatoes. Researchers have also found ways to make crops stronger against stresses like drought and heat.

**Impact** – This work is important because it can provide more nutritious food to billions of people, especially in poor countries where diet lacks key nutrients. Using gene editing can speed up crop improvements compared to traditional breeding. It also helps crops grow better under changing climates, protecting food supplies and health.

**What's next step** – Scientists will continue improving these genetic methods and work with governments to create clear rules for safe use. They aim to bring more biofortified crops to farms worldwide. Combining gene editing with new tools like artificial intelligence could help develop even better crops faster.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Cutting-edge genetic technologies are making crops more nutritious and climate-resilient, offering a practical way to reduce global nutrient deficiencies and support food security.
articles.personality_title_fr: De nouvelles technologies génétiques améliorent la nutrition des cultures pour lutter contre la faim cachée et le changement climatique
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Beaucoup de personnes dans le monde ne reçoivent pas assez de vitamines et de minéraux, un problème appelé la faim cachée. Le changement climatique aggrave cette situation en réduisant la valeur nutritionnelle des cultures de base comme le riz et le blé.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Des scientifiques utilisent des technologies génétiques avancées, notamment l'édition génomique CRISPR-Cas, pour rendre les cultures plus saines. Ces techniques permettent d'ajouter directement des nutriments importants comme les vitamines B, C et A dans des plantes telles que le riz, le blé, la banane et la tomate. Les chercheurs ont aussi trouvé des moyens de rendre les cultures plus résistantes au stress comme la sécheresse et la chaleur.

**Impact** – Ce travail est important car il peut fournir des aliments plus nutritifs à des milliards de personnes, surtout dans les pays pauvres où les régimes alimentaires manquent de nutriments clés. L'édition génomique permet d'améliorer les cultures plus rapidement que les méthodes traditionnelles. Elle aide aussi les cultures à mieux pousser malgré le changement climatique, protégeant ainsi la sécurité alimentaire et la santé.

**Prochaine étape** – Les scientifiques vont continuer à améliorer ces méthodes génétiques et collaborer avec les gouvernements pour établir des règles claires pour leur utilisation en toute sécurité. Ils visent à diffuser davantage de cultures biofortifiées dans le monde. L'association de l'édition génomique avec de nouveaux outils comme l'intelligence artificielle pourrait accélérer le développement de cultures encore meilleures.

**Résumé en une phrase** – Les technologies génétiques de pointe rendent les cultures plus nutritives et résistantes au climat, offrant une solution concrète pour réduire les carences en nutriments dans le monde et renforcer la sécurité alimentaire.
articles.personality_title_es: Nuevas tecnologías genéticas mejoran la nutrición de cultivos para combatir el hambre oculta y el cambio climático
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Muchas personas en el mundo no reciben suficientes vitaminas y minerales en su dieta, un problema llamado hambre oculta. El cambio climático empeora esta situación al reducir el valor nutricional de cultivos básicos como el arroz y el trigo.

**Qué sucedió** – Científicos están utilizando tecnologías genéticas avanzadas, especialmente la edición genética CRISPR-Cas, para hacer que los cultivos sean más saludables. Estas técnicas ayudan a añadir nutrientes importantes como las vitaminas B, C y A directamente en plantas como arroz, trigo, plátano y tomate. También han encontrado formas de hacer que los cultivos resistan mejor el estrés por sequía y calor.

**Impacto** – Este trabajo es importante porque puede proporcionar alimentos más nutritivos a miles de millones de personas, especialmente en países pobres donde la dieta carece de nutrientes clave. La edición genética acelera la mejora de cultivos en comparación con la reproducción tradicional. Además, ayuda a que los cultivos crezcan mejor bajo el cambio climático, protegiendo el suministro de alimentos y la salud.

**Próximo paso** – Los científicos seguirán mejorando estos métodos genéticos y trabajarán con gobiernos para crear normas claras para su uso seguro. Buscan llevar más cultivos biofortificados a los agricultores en todo el mundo. La combinación de la edición genética con herramientas como la inteligencia artificial podría ayudar a desarrollar cultivos aún mejores más rápido.

**Resumen en una frase** – Las tecnologías genéticas avanzadas están haciendo que los cultivos sean más nutritivos y resistentes al clima, ofreciendo una forma práctica de reducir las deficiencias de nutrientes a nivel mundial y apoyar la seguridad alimentaria.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of a flourishing golden rice field under a gentle sky, with stylized glowing kernels symbolizing enhanced vitamins and micronutrients, surrounded by subtle, abstract representations of DNA strands woven into the plant stems, conveying genetic innovation improving crop nutrition amid changing climate conditions.

articles.title: Scientists discover ancient brain cells that help block distractions
articles.summary: Scientists have discovered a tiny group of neurons in an ancient brain region that acts like a built-in focus filter, helping the brain ignore distractions and zero in on what matters most. When researchers temporarily switched off these neurons in mice, the animals became unusually distractible—similar to what is seen in ADHD—but regained normal focus as soon as the neurons were reactivated.
articles.content:
Scientists have identified a group of neurons located in an ancient region of the brain that plays a key role in helping animals focus. These cells appear to improve attention by filtering out distractions and directing the brain toward the most important information.

The discovery, made in mice by researchers at Johns Hopkins University, points to a brain system that is shared by all vertebrates, including humans. The findings could eventually help researchers develop more precise treatments for attention-related disorders.

"A hallmark of ADHD is that even faint distractors draw attention away -- and that's exactly what we see here when these neurons are silenced," said senior author Shreesh Mysore, a neuroscientist who studies neural circuits tied to behavior. "But the very next day, when the neurons are turned back on, the same animal can ignore distractors again, even very strong ones."

The federally funded study was recently published in Nature Communications and selected as an editorial highlight.

Ancient Brain Region Linked to Attention

Humans and other animals constantly sort through competing information, focusing on what matters most while ignoring less important signals. This ability, known as selective spatial attention, allows people to follow a conversation in a noisy room or spot a friend in a crowded space. Difficulties with this process are associated with conditions such as autism and Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD).

For many years, scientists believed that attention was controlled primarily by the prefrontal cortex, a brain region that is especially developed in humans and other primates. However, that explanation leaves an important question unanswered. Many animals can also focus their attention despite lacking a highly developed prefrontal cortex.

"If we really go back in evolution, for hundreds of millions of years, birds have had this ability, fish have had this ability. And they do not typically have a highly developed prefrontal cortex, so how does the brain solve this problem?" said lead author Ninad Kothari, a postdoctoral fellow in the university's Department of Psychological and Brain Sciences. "We were able to identify an evolutionarily old region in the brainstem which affords this ability."

Brainstem Neurons Act as a Focus Filter

The researchers found that attention in mice is also regulated by a network of inhibitory neurons located in the brainstem. These neurons are present across vertebrate species, including birds and fish. The decision to investigate these cells in mice grew out of earlier work by Mysore and other researchers studying birds, frogs, and turtles.

To test the neurons' role, the team designed an attention task similar to those used in human studies. Mice viewed visual cues on a screen and were rewarded when they correctly responded to information displayed directly in front of them while ignoring distracting cues appearing off to the side.

The mice performed the task successfully until researchers temporarily switched off the brainstem neurons.

"When we inactivate these neurons, the mice become hyper distractable," Kothari said.

Distraction Increases When Neurons Are Disabled

The scientists conducted additional tests to determine whether the mice were failing because of vision problems or movement difficulties. Those possibilities were ruled out.

Instead, the experiments showed that the animals specifically lost the ability to evaluate competing information and focus on the most relevant signal.

"The only thing impaired was their ability to take the competing pieces of information, compare them, and pay attention to the location with the most important information," Mysore said. "This part of the brain is like an attentional selection engine. It helps solve the question: 'What is most important information I should pay attention to right now?'"

Potential Implications for ADHD and Autism

The researchers now want to better understand exactly how these neurons influence spatial attention across vertebrate species and whether they serve a similar function in humans.

"All the evidence to date suggests that these neurons exist in humans too," said Mysore. "But are they responsible for selective spatial attention in humans? An exciting hypothesis is that they play a crucial role."

Future studies may examine the activity of these neurons in people with ADHD and autism. If researchers find that the cells function differently in those conditions, the discovery could help guide the development of more targeted medications and therapies.

The study's authors also include Arunima Banerjee, Qingcheng (Jessica) Zhang, and Wen-Kai You of Johns Hopkins University.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant scientific discovery of ancient brain neurons that regulate attention by filtering distractions, a mechanism conserved across vertebrates. This finding has broad implications for understanding and potentially treating attention disorders like ADHD and autism, offering hope for more precise therapies. The study is well-detailed, focused on a single breakthrough with meaningful potential impact on human health.
articles.personality_title: Scientists find ancient brain cells that help block distractions
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Scientists have long studied how animals, including humans, focus their attention despite many distractions. Attention problems are common in conditions like ADHD and autism. Until now, most research focused on the brain’s outer layer, the prefrontal cortex, but animals without this area can still focus well.

**What happened** – Researchers at Johns Hopkins University discovered a small group of neurons deep in the brainstem that helps animals filter out distractions. They tested this in mice by turning these neurons off, which made the mice lose focus and become easily distracted. When the neurons were turned back on, the mice quickly regained their ability to concentrate.

**Impact** – This discovery shows that a very old part of the brain, shared by many animals including humans, controls attention by acting like a filter. It explains how animals without a developed prefrontal cortex can still focus. Understanding these neurons better could lead to new treatments for attention disorders by targeting this brain system more precisely.

**What's next step** – Scientists plan to study if these neurons work the same way in humans and if they behave differently in people with ADHD or autism. This could help develop better medicines or therapies that improve attention by focusing on these specific brain cells.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Researchers have found ancient brain cells that act like a focus filter, helping animals ignore distractions and pointing to new ways to treat attention problems.
articles.personality_title_fr: Des scientifiques découvrent des cellules cérébrales anciennes qui bloquent les distractions
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les scientifiques cherchent depuis longtemps à comprendre comment les animaux, y compris les humains, concentrent leur attention malgré de nombreuses distractions. Les troubles de l’attention sont fréquents dans des conditions comme le TDAH et l’autisme. Jusqu’à présent, la recherche se concentrait surtout sur le cortex préfrontal, mais certains animaux sans cette zone peuvent aussi bien se concentrer.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Des chercheurs de l’Université Johns Hopkins ont découvert un petit groupe de neurones dans le tronc cérébral qui aide les animaux à filtrer les distractions. Ils ont testé cela chez des souris en désactivant ces neurones, ce qui a rendu les souris facilement distraites. Quand les neurones ont été réactivés, les souris ont rapidement retrouvé leur capacité de concentration.

**Impact** – Cette découverte montre qu’une partie très ancienne du cerveau, partagée par de nombreux animaux y compris les humains, contrôle l’attention en agissant comme un filtre. Elle explique comment des animaux sans cortex préfrontal développé peuvent quand même se concentrer. Mieux comprendre ces neurones pourrait mener à de nouveaux traitements plus précis des troubles de l’attention.

**Prochaine étape** – Les scientifiques veulent étudier si ces neurones fonctionnent de la même manière chez l’humain et s’ils sont différents chez les personnes atteintes de TDAH ou d’autisme. Cela pourrait aider à développer de meilleurs médicaments ou thérapies ciblant ces cellules cérébrales.

**Phrase clé** – Des chercheurs ont trouvé des cellules cérébrales anciennes qui agissent comme un filtre d’attention, aidant les animaux à ignorer les distractions et ouvrant la voie à de nouveaux traitements des troubles de l’attention.
articles.personality_title_es: Científicos descubren células cerebrales antiguas que bloquean las distracciones
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Durante mucho tiempo, los científicos han estudiado cómo los animales, incluidos los humanos, pueden concentrarse a pesar de muchas distracciones. Los problemas de atención son comunes en condiciones como el TDAH y el autismo. Hasta ahora, la mayoría de la investigación se centraba en la corteza prefrontal, pero algunos animales sin esta zona también pueden concentrarse bien.

**Qué pasó** – Investigadores de la Universidad Johns Hopkins descubrieron un pequeño grupo de neuronas en el tronco cerebral que ayuda a los animales a filtrar las distracciones. Lo probaron en ratones apagando estas neuronas, lo que hizo que los ratones se distrajeran fácilmente. Cuando las neuronas se volvieron a activar, los ratones recuperaron rápidamente su capacidad de concentración.

**Impacto** – Este descubrimiento muestra que una parte muy antigua del cerebro, compartida por muchos animales, incluidos los humanos, controla la atención actuando como un filtro. Explica cómo animales sin corteza prefrontal desarrollada pueden concentrarse. Entender mejor estas neuronas podría llevar a nuevos tratamientos más precisos para los trastornos de atención.

**Próximo paso** – Los científicos planean estudiar si estas neuronas funcionan igual en humanos y si se comportan diferente en personas con TDAH o autismo. Esto podría ayudar a desarrollar mejores medicamentos o terapias que mejoren la atención enfocándose en estas células cerebrales.

**Frase clave** – Los investigadores encontraron células cerebrales antiguas que actúan como un filtro de concentración, ayudando a los animales a ignorar distracciones y señalando nuevas formas de tratar problemas de atención.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of a serene mouse attentively focusing on a glowing, clear path of light ahead while subtle, shadowy shapes representing distractions gently fade away to the sides, with an ancient, softly illuminated brainstem structure subtly integrated into the background as a symbolic filter guiding the mouse’s attention.

articles.title: Scientists discover hidden “footprints of death” that may help viruses spread
articles.summary: Scientists have uncovered a surprising new twist in what happens when cells die. As dying cells break apart, they leave behind tiny “footprints of death” packed with newly discovered particles that help guide the immune system to clean up the remains. But researchers found that influenza viruses can exploit this process, hiding inside these microscopic packages and potentially using them to spread to nearby cells.
articles.content:
Researchers at La Trobe University have identified a previously unknown biological process that may help explain how viruses move through the body. The discovery could also open new opportunities for developing treatments that better support the immune system.

Published in Nature Communications, the study sheds new light on what happens when cells die and are removed from the body.

The research was led by PhD candidate Stephanie Rutter in the laboratory of Professor Ivan Poon at the La Trobe Institute for Molecular Science (LIMS). The team found that the steps involved in cell death are far more organized and important than scientists previously realized.

Newly Discovered Cell Death Vesicles

As cells reach the end of their life cycle and begin to self-destruct, they undergo a series of physical changes. The researchers observed that dying cells alter their shape, detach from nearby structures, and leave behind a residue they call "the footprint of death."

Within this residue, the team discovered a previously unknown type of Extracellular Vesicle (EV).

EVs are microscopic particles released by cells that carry proteins, lipids, DNA and RNA to other cells. They play an essential role in cell-to-cell communication throughout the body.

The newly identified vesicles, called F-ApoEVs, remain at the site where a cell has died. They act like breadcrumb trails that help the immune system locate and remove cellular debris before it can trigger unwanted inflammation.

How Viruses Exploit the Cleanup Process

The researchers also uncovered an unexpected twist.

In laboratory experiments involving influenza-infected cells, they found that viruses could take advantage of this natural cleanup mechanism. Viral particles were able to hide inside the F-ApoEVs, potentially allowing infection to spread to neighboring cells while remaining concealed within the body's normal disposal process.

Professor Poon, Director of the Research Centre for Extracellular Vesicles (RCEV), said the findings may have important implications for future therapies.

"Understanding this basic biological process could open new avenues of research to develop new treatments that harness these steps and help the immune system better fight disease," Professor Poon said.

"Billions of cells are programmed to die each day as a part of normal turnover and disease progression, and until now, it was believed that the cell fragmentation process during cell death was random and fairly simple.

"Our findings demonstrate the complexity of this process and highlight how each step in the process is actually critical to help the dying cell break down efficiently and to be cleared away by the immune system."

Cell Communication After Death

Lead researcher and PhD candidate Stephanie Rutter said the findings highlight the importance of communication between cells and show how viruses can manipulate these biological systems.

"We know that the body clears away dead cell fragments to prevent them lingering and causing inflammation and autoimmune diseases such as Systemic Lupus Erythematosis (SLE), and we saw F-ApoEVs are readily cleared from the site of cell death," Stephanie said.

"What we didn't expect was how viruses can also take advantage of this process and cause infection by hiding in F-ApoEVs."

The team believes the discovery could eventually improve understanding of both infectious diseases and autoimmune disorders, leading to new treatment strategies.

"The more we can understand about cell death and what happens to cells after they die, the better we can understand disease pathologies and find new treatments," Stephanie said.

New Insights Into Immune Function

Study co-leader Dr. Georgia Atkin-Smith of WEHI said understanding how dying cells communicate with the immune system is important because cell death plays a role in a wide range of diseases.

"This study has revealed that dying cells can continue to communicate from the grave and may impact immune function," Dr. Atkin-Smith said.

The research was carried out by scientists from La Trobe University's RCEV, LIMS and the School of Agriculture, Biomedicine and Environment (SABE). The project was conducted in collaboration with researchers at WEHI and Toronto Metropolitan University in Canada.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant scientific discovery about cell death and immune system communication that has broad implications for understanding infectious diseases and autoimmune disorders, potentially leading to new treatments. The research is detailed, focused on a single topic, and highlights a breakthrough with meaningful real-world health impact.
articles.personality_title: Scientists find new cell death particles that viruses use to spread
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Scientists have long studied how cells die and are removed from the body. Cell death is a normal process that helps keep us healthy by clearing old or damaged cells. However, the details of how dying cells communicate with the immune system were not fully understood.

**What happened** – Researchers at La Trobe University discovered tiny particles called F-ApoEVs that are left behind when cells die. These particles act like markers that help the immune system find and clean up dead cells. But the team also found that influenza viruses can hide inside these particles, using them to sneak into nearby healthy cells and spread infection.

**Impact** – This discovery changes how we understand cell death and virus infections. It shows that cell death is a carefully organized process, not random as previously thought. Knowing that viruses can exploit this cleanup system could lead to new ways to stop infections. It also helps explain how the body manages inflammation and autoimmune diseases.

**What's next step** – Scientists hope to study these F-ApoEV particles more to find treatments that help the immune system fight viruses better. Future research may also explore how this process affects other diseases, possibly leading to new medicines for infections and autoimmune conditions.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Scientists uncovered new particles left by dying cells that guide immune cleanup but can also help viruses spread, opening paths for better disease treatments.
articles.personality_title_fr: Les scientifiques découvrent de nouvelles particules de mort cellulaire utilisées par les virus pour se propager
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les scientifiques étudient depuis longtemps comment les cellules meurent et sont éliminées du corps. La mort cellulaire est un processus normal qui permet de maintenir la santé en supprimant les cellules vieilles ou endommagées. Cependant, les détails sur la communication des cellules mourantes avec le système immunitaire n’étaient pas complètement compris.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Des chercheurs de l’Université La Trobe ont découvert de minuscules particules appelées F-ApoEV laissées par les cellules en train de mourir. Ces particules servent de marqueurs qui aident le système immunitaire à localiser et nettoyer les cellules mortes. Mais l’équipe a aussi trouvé que les virus de la grippe peuvent se cacher à l’intérieur de ces particules, les utilisant pour infecter les cellules voisines.

**Impact** – Cette découverte change notre compréhension de la mort cellulaire et des infections virales. Elle montre que la mort des cellules est un processus organisé, et non aléatoire. Savoir que les virus exploitent ce système de nettoyage pourrait aider à développer de nouveaux moyens pour stopper les infections. Cela explique aussi comment le corps gère l’inflammation et les maladies auto-immunes.

**Prochaine étape** – Les scientifiques souhaitent étudier davantage les particules F-ApoEV pour trouver des traitements qui renforcent la lutte du système immunitaire contre les virus. Les recherches futures pourraient aussi explorer l’impact de ce processus sur d’autres maladies, menant à de nouveaux médicaments.

**Résumé en une phrase** – Des scientifiques ont découvert de nouvelles particules laissées par les cellules mourantes qui guident le nettoyage immunitaire mais peuvent aussi aider les virus à se propager, ouvrant la voie à de meilleurs traitements.
articles.personality_title_es: Científicos descubren nuevas partículas de muerte celular que los virus usan para propagarse
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Los científicos han estudiado durante mucho tiempo cómo mueren las células y cómo el cuerpo las elimina. La muerte celular es un proceso normal que ayuda a mantener la salud al eliminar células viejas o dañadas. Sin embargo, no se entendían completamente los detalles de cómo las células moribundas se comunican con el sistema inmunológico.

**Qué pasó** – Investigadores de la Universidad La Trobe descubrieron partículas diminutas llamadas F-ApoEV que quedan cuando las células mueren. Estas partículas actúan como señales que ayudan al sistema inmunológico a encontrar y limpiar las células muertas. Pero también encontraron que los virus de la influenza pueden esconderse dentro de estas partículas para infectar células cercanas.

**Impacto** – Este hallazgo cambia la forma en que entendemos la muerte celular y las infecciones virales. Demuestra que la muerte celular es un proceso organizado, no al azar. Saber que los virus pueden aprovechar este sistema de limpieza podría ayudar a crear nuevas formas de detener infecciones. También ayuda a entender cómo el cuerpo controla la inflamación y las enfermedades autoinmunes.

**Próximo paso** – Los científicos esperan estudiar más las partículas F-ApoEV para desarrollar tratamientos que ayuden al sistema inmunológico a combatir mejor los virus. La investigación futura también podría explorar cómo este proceso afecta otras enfermedades, llevando a nuevos medicamentos.

**Conclusión en una frase** – Científicos descubrieron nuevas partículas dejadas por células moribundas que guían la limpieza inmunológica pero que también pueden ayudar a los virus a propagarse, abriendo caminos para mejores tratamientos.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of glowing, delicate cellular vesicles shaped like soft, translucent footprints gently scattered across a textured biological landscape, with subtle, shadowy virus-like particles nestled discreetly inside some vesicles, symbolizing the hidden viral spread within the natural cleanup process of dying cells.

articles.title: AI tool spots antibiotics that fight drug-resistant gonorrhoea
articles.summary: Nature, Published online: 24 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01987-7The bacterium Neisseria gonorrhoeae has evolved resistance to most antibiotics used to treat it, but a machine-learning screen reveals potential therapies.
articles.content: Thank you for visiting nature.com. You are using a browser version with limited support for CSS. To obtain the best experience, we recommend you use a more up to date browser (or turn off compatibility mode in Internet Explorer). In the meantime, to ensure continued support, we are displaying the site without styles and JavaScript.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant breakthrough where an AI tool has identified potential new antibiotics to combat drug-resistant gonorrhoea, addressing a major public health challenge with broad implications for antibiotic resistance and infectious disease treatment.
articles.personality_title: AI discovers new antibiotics to fight drug-resistant gonorrhoea
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Gonorrhoea is a common sexually transmitted infection caused by the bacterium Neisseria gonorrhoeae. Over time, this bacterium has become resistant to many antibiotics, making it harder to treat.

**What happened** – Scientists used an artificial intelligence (AI) tool to search through many possible medicines. This AI helped find new antibiotics that might work against the drug-resistant gonorrhoea. The discovery was announced in June 2026.

**Impact** – This is important because drug-resistant gonorrhoea is a growing problem worldwide. Finding new antibiotics means doctors may soon have better ways to treat patients when older medicines no longer work. It also shows how AI can help solve tough medical challenges.

**What's next step** – Researchers will now test these new antibiotics in labs and then in clinical trials to make sure they are safe and effective for people. If successful, these drugs could become new treatments for gonorrhoea.

**One-sentence takeaway** – An AI tool has found promising new antibiotics that could help treat gonorrhoea infections resistant to current medicines.
articles.personality_title_fr: L’IA découvre de nouveaux antibiotiques contre la gonorrhée résistante aux médicaments
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – La gonorrhée est une infection sexuellement transmissible causée par la bactérie Neisseria gonorrhoeae. Cette bactérie est devenue résistante à de nombreux antibiotiques, ce qui complique son traitement.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Des scientifiques ont utilisé un outil d’intelligence artificielle (IA) pour examiner de nombreux médicaments possibles. Cette IA a aidé à trouver de nouveaux antibiotiques efficaces contre la gonorrhée résistante. Cette découverte a été annoncée en juin 2026.

**Impact** – C’est important car la gonorrhée résistante aux médicaments est un problème mondial croissant. Trouver de nouveaux antibiotiques signifie que les médecins auront bientôt de meilleures options pour soigner les patients lorsque les anciens médicaments ne fonctionnent plus. Cela montre aussi comment l’IA peut aider à résoudre des défis médicaux complexes.

**Prochaine étape** – Les chercheurs vont maintenant tester ces nouveaux antibiotiques en laboratoire, puis dans des essais cliniques, pour vérifier qu’ils sont sûrs et efficaces pour les humains. Si tout va bien, ces médicaments pourraient devenir de nouveaux traitements contre la gonorrhée.

**Une phrase clé** – Un outil d’IA a découvert des antibiotiques prometteurs pour traiter la gonorrhée résistante aux traitements actuels.
articles.personality_title_es: La IA descubre nuevos antibióticos para combatir la gonorrea resistente a medicamentos
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – La gonorrea es una infección de transmisión sexual causada por la bacteria Neisseria gonorrhoeae. Esta bacteria se ha vuelto resistente a muchos antibióticos, lo que dificulta su tratamiento.

**Qué pasó** – Científicos usaron una herramienta de inteligencia artificial (IA) para buscar entre muchos medicamentos posibles. Esta IA ayudó a encontrar nuevos antibióticos que podrían funcionar contra la gonorrea resistente a medicamentos. El descubrimiento se anunció en junio de 2026.

**Impacto** – Esto es importante porque la gonorrea resistente a medicamentos es un problema creciente en todo el mundo. Encontrar nuevos antibióticos significa que los médicos podrían tener mejores opciones para tratar a los pacientes cuando los medicamentos antiguos ya no funcionan. También muestra cómo la IA puede ayudar a resolver problemas médicos difíciles.

**Próximo paso** – Los investigadores ahora probarán estos nuevos antibióticos en laboratorios y luego en ensayos clínicos para asegurarse de que sean seguros y efectivos para las personas. Si tienen éxito, estos medicamentos podrían convertirse en nuevos tratamientos para la gonorrea.

**Una frase clave** – Una herramienta de IA ha encontrado nuevos antibióticos prometedores para tratar infecciones de gonorrea resistentes a los medicamentos actuales.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm painting of a glowing, stylized AI brain network gently illuminating a cluster of abstract, shield-like antibiotic molecules that are breaking apart dark, spiky representations of drug-resistant bacteria, all set against a soft, natural color background symbolizing hope and scientific breakthrough.
